riskClass: auth or identity; permission, secret, or trust-boundary logic
workDescription: ADM-012 (issue #142) — web-first staff account setup. Repoints the ADM-011 staff-invite email link from the mobile deep-link redirect (`/staff-invite/native`) to a new web accept page (`apps/admin/src/routes/staff-invite-accept.tsx`, ${ADMIN_WEB_ORIGIN}/staff-invite-accept). After the existing magic-link-session-then-consume flow lands a session and applies the invite's STORED role/branch (unchanged from ADM-011), the web page adds two new required onboarding sub-steps run against that already-authenticated session: (1) a Profile step (name/birthday/address via authClient.updateUser, byte-identical call shape to mobile customer onboarding, role structurally excluded server-side via input:false) and (2) a Password step backed by a new session-gated route, POST /staff-invite/set-password, which reuses better-auth's auth.api.setPassword verbatim against the CALLER'S OWN session only (no target-user-id parameter exists anywhere in the request). PASSWORD_ALREADY_SET is treated as a graceful 200 no-op, not an error, and never mutates an existing credential. After both steps, the page branches purely on the ALREADY-CAPTURED /consume response role (no new privilege read): admin/super_admin route to the dashboard, staff get a terminal 'sign in on the app' card with no dashboard-access affordance. Mobile's (auth)/_layout.tsx had its invite-accept Stack.Screen line removed (route de-registration only; the screen file itself is byte-unmodified). Zero changes to POST /api/admin/users/:id/role or PATCH /api/admin/staff/:id/branch (both remain byte-frozen, confirmed by diff). No new privilege grant is introduced by ADM-012 itself — the role/branch grant remains entirely ADM-011's /staff-invite/consume; ADM-012 only adds credential-provisioning (password) and profile completion on top of an already-granted session, plus a routing decision that reads (never re-derives) that already-granted role.
approver: orchestrator-driven adversarial re-analysis of the committed ADM-012 diff (commit 81974a9) — this evidence pack was generated post-EXECUTE/EVL, not during VALIDATE; see adversarial-validation.json for the attack-scenario analysis this classification rests on
note: Distinct from ADM-011's own harness/ pack (same feature folder's adm-011-add-staff_21-07-26/harness/) — that pack covers the invite-create + promote + consume surface. This pack covers ONLY ADM-012's new surface: set-password, the web accept page's profile/password/routing steps, and the acceptUrl repoint. Where ADM-012 reuses an ADM-011 guarantee unmodified (e.g. the role/branch grant itself, the atomic single-use consume), this pack cites that guarantee as already-proven rather than re-deriving it.
